data.title: Trump Tax Bill Will Increase Deficit By $2.4Bn
data.description:
Catenaa, Wednesday, June 04, 2025– President Donald Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill will cut taxes by $3. 75 trillion and increase the deficit by $2. 4 trillion over the next decade, the Congressional Budget Office(CBO) said on Wednesday. According to the nonpartisan CBO, the number of people without health insurance is projected to increase by 10. 9 million under […]
source: https://investors.catenaa.com/news/trump-tax-bill-will-increase-deficit-by-2-4bn

data.title: Warren Targets GOP’s Crypto Wallet Loophole in GENIUS Act – Will Her Amendment Block Trump’s Wallet Ambitions?
data.description:
Elizabeth Warren has moved to tighten the GENIUS Act, proposing an amendment barring sitting or former presidents and their families from profiting via stablecoins or wallet platforms. She says a late exemption could let Donald Trump’s USD1 venture avoid federal oversight.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/genius-act-crypto-wallet-loophole/

data.title: BlackRock Bitcoin Futures ETF Launches on Moscow Exchange Amid Privacy Concerns
data.description:
The Moscow Exchange has added BlackRock’s IBIT-9. 25 Bitcoin Futures ETF, giving qualified investors access while full compliance checks start on June 23. Yet Rosfinmonitoring has noted that crypto exchanges share wallet data with police, challenging privacy hopes in Russia’s developing crypto market
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/blackrock-bitcoin-futures-etf-launch-moscow-exchange/

data.title: JPMorgan Chase to accept cryptocurrency ETFs as loan collateral
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News JPMorgan Chase is preparing to accept cryptocurrency-related assets as collateral for loans, including Bitcoin exchange-traded funds. The bank will first provide financing services for cryptocurrency ETFs, with the first batch involving BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust. The move marks a major expansion of JPMorgan Chase's cryptocurrency-related business, and comes at a time when the overall regulatory environment is loosening under the new US government. 
Previously, JPMorgan Chase accepted such collateral only on a limited case-by-case basis. But under the new plan, cryptocurrency ETFs will be treated more like traditional assets (such as stocks or real estate) when assessing customers' borrowing capacity. The report also pointed out that the plan, which is expected to be launched in the coming weeks, may include more cryptocurrency investment products. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Breakthrough: JPMorgan to Offer Crypto Services Despite Jamie Dimon’s Past Criticism
data.description:
(NYSE: JPM), a top-tier financial institution with a sprawling global presence, is expanding its crypto offerings led by Bitcoin (BTC). According to a report by Bloomberg, JPMorgan, which manages more than $4. 3 trillion in customers’ assets, plans to consider Bitcoin and other digital assets when assessing clients’ overall net worth
source: https://coinpedia.org/news/bitcoin-breakthrough-jpmorgan-to-offer-crypto-services-despite-jamie-dimons-past-criticism/

data.title: Eurozone set to expand as EU greenlights Bulgaria’s accession
data.description:
The eurozone is about to grow in territory and influence following formal approvals by key EU institutions for accepting Bulgaria as its newest member. The Eastern European nation is on its way to become the 21st country using the common currency in 2026. However, the prospects of joining the eurozone have been causing political turmoil […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/eurozone-set-to-expand-as-eu-greenlights-bulgarias-accession/

data.title: Federal Reserve Beige Book: Economic activity declined slightly
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Federal Reserve Beige Book: Prices have risen at a moderate pace since the last report. However, a few district reports show that the economic outlook has deteriorated, while others show that the outlook has improved. Reports from twelve Federal Reserve districts show that economic activity has declined slightly since the last report. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Beige Book: Wages continue to grow at a moderate pace
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Federal Reserve Beige Book said that employment conditions have remained largely unchanged since the last report. Most regions reported flat employment, 3 regions reported slight to moderate employment growth, and 2 regions reported a slight decline in employment. Many regions reported lower employee turnover and more applicants for open positions. Comments about uncertainty leading to delayed hiring were widespread. All regions cited a decline in labor demand, citing reasons including reduced work hours and overtime, hiring pauses, and layoff plans. Some regions reported layoffs in certain industries, but these layoffs were not widespread. Two regions noted that for many respondents, hiring plans had not changed since the beginning of the year. Wages continued to grow at a moderate pace, although many regions reported a general easing of wage pressures. A few regions said that rising costs of living continued to put upward pressure on wages. (Jinshi)
